_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: E-invoice exemption walked back twice in a month after Sabah loss
context: On 6 December 2025, a week after Pakatan Harapan's Sabah election defeat, PM Anwar raised the e-invoice exemption from RM500,000 to RM1m turnover. On 5 January 2026 the RM1m-5m penalty-free transition was extended to end-2026. Phase 1-2 compliance was already over 90%.

cards.text: On 6 December 2025, a week after Pakatan Harapan's Sabah election loss, PM Anwar raised the e-invoice exemption to RM1m turnover.
cards.sub: SAMENTA called it relief for about 200,000 SMEs; ACCCIM put the number at 700,000-800,000. Reporting linked the timing to PH's urban vote losses in Sabah.

cards.h: Government estimates put Malaysia's shadow economy near 21% of GDP, around RM330 billion. Other methodologies range from 7% to 26.6%.
cards.s: Khazanah's narrower 'informal sector' frame gives 4-7.8% (2019). World Economics currently reports 26.6%. The range reflects definition, not measurement.
cards.d: Government estimates put Malaysia's shadow economy near 21% of GDP, around RM330 billion. Other methodologies range from 7% to 26.6%. Khazanah's narrower 'informal sector' frame gives 4-7.8% (2019). World Economics currently reports 26.6%. The range reflects definition, not measurement.

cards.h: By 22 December 2025 LHDN had logged 820.5 million e-invoices from 111,600 taxpayers: Phase 1-2 compliance over 90%, Phase 3 above 80%.
cards.s: Volume more than doubled from 328m in June 2025. By 27 April 2026 LHDN said e-invoice checks had surfaced RM3.5 billion in undeclared income.
cards.d: By 22 December 2025 LHDN had logged 820.5 million e-invoices from 111,600 taxpayers: Phase 1-2 compliance over 90%, Phase 3 above 80%. Volume more than doubled from 328m in June 2025. By 27 April 2026 LHDN said e-invoice checks had surfaced RM3.5 billion in undeclared income.

cards.h: On 5 January 2026 the penalty-free transition for RM1m-5m firms was extended to end-2026. On 20 April 2026 MOF extended it again to end-2027.
cards.s: LHDN's July 2025 e-invoice checks had already flagged 5,800 taxpayers for RM484m undeclared prior-year income, recovering about RM82m in additional tax.
cards.d: On 5 January 2026 the penalty-free transition for RM1m-5m firms was extended to end-2026. On 20 April 2026 MOF extended it again to end-2027. LHDN's July 2025 e-invoice checks had already flagged 5,800 taxpayers for RM484m undeclared prior-year income, recovering about RM82m in additional tax.

cards.text: Phase 1-2 compliance: over 90%. Seven days from Sabah loss to first exemption. The system scales where enforced; the rule moves where political pressure shows.
E-invoicing works where enforced: 820m invoices, >90% Phase 1-2 compliance. Micro-firm cost relief is legitimate. But the rule walks back faster than the shadow economy shrinks.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
